title: Post-Bid GC Awarded but Didn't Pull a Wenger Number
openingLine: Yeah, we got awarded the Pinewood project last week. I know your stuff was in the spec — pit filler — but we didn't pull a number from you, we used Stage Pro. Is there something you needed from us?
customerContext: You're a busy PM who just won a big award. You used a competitor on the pit filler because your estimator had their number from a previous project and didn't want to chase a new quote with the bid clock running. You're not against Wenger — you just took the path of least resistance. You're a little annoyed that a sales rep is calling you about a bid that's already over.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Estimator had Stage Pro's number from a previous bid. Easier to use it than chase a new one.
We haven't talked to the owner about it. They saw the spec and assumed we used it.
Wenger was approved in the bid docs but not basis of design — the spec said 'Wenger or approved equal.'
Contract isn't signed yet. We could swap if there's a real reason to.
If you can match $87K and prove it's a real apples-to-apples, I'd consider it.
School board approved the project. Music director was in the design meetings.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Reached out to the GC promptly
Asked why they didn't take a Wenger number
Asked about owner-side communication and preferences

Solution Framing.behavior: Requested an opportunity to provide post-bid pricing

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Contacted owner about possible VE or direct-buy pathway

BANT.behavior: Confirmed contract status (signed vs. not signed)
Asked for commitment if pricing is met

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Confirmed next steps and timing with the GC

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Established a clear next step before the call ended

Other.behavior: Documented LOST competitive comments in Salesforce
